title: Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser
content:
{{Top|canon=Mk I Superlaser}} {{Weapon |image=[[File:Superlaser2.jpg]] |name=Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser |manufacturer=[[Imperial Department of Military Research/Legends|Imperial Department of Military Research]] |model=Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser |type= |culture= |creators= |created= |destroyed= |discovered= |owners= |locations= |cost=200,000,000,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] |value= |shape= |size= |material= |length= |width= |diam= |height= |weight= |protection= |crew= |capacity= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose= |heritage= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|Juno, where am I?''"
"''Some sort of convergence array. According to my schematics, there are seven others just like it spread around the station's dish. The firing tube you were in is just one of sixty-four, all being directed into a single, massive beam. I can't even imagine what the Empire could do with this sort of power.|[[Galen Marek]] and [[Juno Eclipse/Legends|Juno Eclipse]] during the [[Battle on Death Star I]]|''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''}} The '''Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser''' was the type of [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] used on the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Stars]]. ==Description== [[File:Dsiipown.png|thumb|left|250px|The Death Star II's superlaser with seven tributary beams.]] The [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star's]] superlaser had eight [[tributary beam]]s arranged in a circle around the concave dish. The [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star's]] superlaser had seven tributary beams arranged around the concave dish in a heptagonal array encompassed by a circle, and an eighth beam in the middle. These beams would then combine to form the main superlaser by the usage of an invisible forcefield in the shape of a cone. The tributary beams were themselves composed of eight smaller beams, which converged in one of the eight Superlaser Convergence Chambers, totaling 64 beam tubes. At the absolute center of the concave dish superlaser were focusing lenses that allowed for the superlaser to be intensified. Originally, the Death Star's superlaser could only fire at planets and required hours to recharge the superlaser.''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' By the time the Death Star II was built, the superlaser was able to fire on large [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and required a much lower recharge time, a matter of minutes. The power source of the laser were special power crystals that the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] took from [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeeto]] on the orders of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]. In addition, activation of the Death Star's superlaser was particularly costly to Imperial military, both in terms of finances as well as losing any potential strategic resources in a habitable or resource-rich planet, and thus [[IMQ-54544-XX-07 Security Clearance Black DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|various restrictions]] were put into place for the use of the superlaser: A full and current [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] report on a target is required before activating the superlaser; targeting even listed worlds without the explicit authorization of [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial High Command]] and the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] is explicitly forbidden; and only personnel with oversector administrative rank and current firing codes provided by the [[Office of the Emperor]] were authorized with activating the superlaser.''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' ==History== The first full-scale superlaser was installed in the first Death Star [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], becoming its primary weapon. However, it ran into technical difficulties during its development, resulting in the proposition and potential development of the [[Composite Beam Superlaser]] as an alternative in the event that the Concave Dish's technical difficulties were far too severe to allow for creation. This weapon had to be manually moved into position by a mechanical arm, and fired proton beams at the targeted planet to rupture the planet's core and render it uninhabitable. Eventually, the Concave Model was chosen for the final design for the superlaser weapon, beating out the Composite Beam Superlaser regular model while a [[Unidentified Death Star architect|weapons researcher]] oversaw development in [[Bevel Lemelisk]]'s stead.''[[Death Star Designer]]'' At some point during its development, there had been plans to incorporate the Superlaser on the equator of the battlestation.''[[Death Star Owner's Technical Manual]]'' In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], [[Galen Marek]] traveled through one of the firing tubes in the superlaser dish during the [[Battle on Death Star I]], causing structural damage in the process. During this time, the Imperials were also conducting several test fires at one-minute intervals, forcing Marek to hide from the superlaser when it was fired, and also killing several Imperials unlucky enough to get caught in the blasts during their search for Marek. [[File:Superlaser-blast.png|thumb|right|250px|One of the tributary beams travels through the beam tube on the Death Star]] The full-scale superlaser was built in the first Death Star and was used six times—it was first used during a Rebel raid on the Death Star, when Rebel agent [[X2]] used the yet-unfinished superlaser to destroy one of the escorting Star Destroyers, then it was fired once with four percent power to destroy the ''[[Fortressa]]'', a [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class battleship]] under Rebel control, three shots were fired as a secret test shortly after, to [[Destruction of Despayre|destroy]] the prison planet [[Despayre]], and another shot with full power to [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroy]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] and demonstrate the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] strength. This immense power made the Death Star one of the most powerful weapons in the [[Timeline of galactic history/Legends|history]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. However, before it could fire a seventh time at the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Great Temple/Legends|base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], it was destroyed at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. An improved version was built alongside the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]], which had been responsible for destroying the [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']] and the bulk cruiser ''[[Urjani]]''. However, because of the Rebel Fleet forcing various ships within Death Squadron into a [[broadside battle]], it wasn't able to show much use by that point. It was also intended to be used to destroy Endor in the event that the Rebels successfully destroyed the shield generator, although the Death Star II, alongside the concave dish composite beam superlaser, ended up destroyed before it could get a chance to do so. ==Behind the scenes== In the StarWars Blu-ray special edition collection section both the first and second Death Star's Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser area are called the "Laser eye." It was also originally planned to be in the middle of the [[Equatorial trench]], as seen in the plans. ==Appearances== *{{Film|II}} {{Hologram}} *''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' *{{Film|III}} *''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'' *[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] *''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'' *{{Film|IV}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] *{{Film|VI}} *[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi (junior novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'' junior novelization]] ===Non-canon appearances=== *''[[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy]]'' *''[[Death Star Designer]]'' {{1stID|Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser}} *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' ==Sources== {{MoreSources}} *''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'' *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Rayo Superláser de Parabólica Cóncava }} [[Category:Death Star II locations]] [[Category:DS-1 Orbital Battle Station locations]] [[Category:Imperial Department of Military Research products]] [[Category:Superlasers]]
